title: 65 By ANT
description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 65 participants during a nociceptive laser stimulation paradigm with subjective pain rating. Participants received 30 laser stimuli at individually calibrated intensities and provided verbal pain ratings on a 0-10 scale. The study investigates the neural correlates of pain perception and subjective pain intensity assessment.
methods_description: Participants underwent 30 laser stimulations at individually calibrated energy intensities (corresponding to an average pain rating of 7). Following each stimulus, participants provided verbal pain ratings on a scale from 0 (no sensation) to 10 (unbearable pain). Participants were instructed to maintain fixation on a crosshair while keeping eyes open. Intertrial intervals were randomized at approximately 5 seconds following pain rating. Laser markers in the EEG data are delayed by 100ms.
